forks.name: Osaka

architecture.overview: Osaka extends the executable EL spec with stricter transaction caps, blob fee bounds, new CLZ opcode, secp256r1 precompile, and requests hashing so the consensus layer can audit deposits and custody-linked payloads through Engine API exchanges. [S1][S2] Sources: [S1] https://github.com/ethereum/execution-specs/blob/5ddb904fa7ba27daeff423e78466744c51e8cb6a/src/ethereum/forks/osaka/__init__.py, [S2] https://github.com/ethereum/execution-specs/blob/5ddb904fa7ba27daeff423e78466744c51e8cb6a/src/ethereum/forks/osaka/fork.py

components.name: State Transition
components.description: Applies header validation, enforced RLP block size, blob gas accounting, withdrawals, and request hashing before mutating state roots. [S1] Sources: [S1] https://github.com/ethereum/execution-specs/blob/5ddb904fa7ba27daeff423e78466744c51e8cb6a/src/ethereum/forks/osaka/fork.py

components.name: Transaction Validation
components.description: Checks Osaka intrinsic gas floor, 2**24 gas cap, nonce bounds, calldata pricing, and blob bundle integrity prior to execution. [S1] Sources: [S1] https://github.com/ethereum/execution-specs/blob/5ddb904fa7ba27daeff423e78466744c51e8cb6a/src/ethereum/forks/osaka/transactions.py

components.name: Requests Pipeline
components.description: Extracts deposit, withdrawal, and consolidation requests from receipts, encodes them, and computes the block-level requests_hash exposed to the CL. [S1][S2] Sources: [S1] https://github.com/ethereum/execution-specs/blob/5ddb904fa7ba27daeff423e78466744c51e8cb6a/src/ethereum/forks/osaka/requests.py, [S2] https://github.com/ethereum/execution-specs/blob/5ddb904fa7ba27daeff423e78466744c51e8cb6a/src/ethereum/forks/osaka/fork.py

components.name: Precompile Suite
components.description: Implements P256VERIFY with strict field bounds, constant 6,900 gas charge, and 160-byte input contract to validate secp256r1 signatures without reverts. [S1] Sources: [S1] https://github.com/ethereum/execution-specs/blob/5ddb904fa7ba27daeff423e78466744c51e8cb6a/src/ethereum/forks/osaka/vm/precompiled_contracts/p256verify.py

state_machines.name: Block Execution
inputs: Ordered transactions with blob sidecars [S1] Sources: [S1] https://github.com/ethereum/execution-specs/blob/5ddb904fa7ba27daeff423e78466744c51e8cb6a/src/ethereum/forks/osaka/fork.py
Parent header fields and requests list [S1] Sources: [S1] https://github.com/ethereum/execution-specs/blob/5ddb904fa7ba27daeff423e78466744c51e8cb6a/src/ethereum/forks/osaka/fork.py
outputs: Receipts root and logs bloom [S1] Sources: [S1] https://github.com/ethereum/execution-specs/blob/5ddb904fa7ba27daeff423e78466744c51e8cb6a/src/ethereum/forks/osaka/fork.py
invariants: Total gas used must not exceed the header gas limit or the block is invalid. [S1] Sources: [S1] https://github.com/ethereum/execution-specs/blob/5ddb904fa7ba27daeff423e78466744c51e8cb6a/src/ethereum/forks/osaka/fork.py

Blob gas used must match header blob_gas_used and stay within MAX_BLOB_GAS_PER_BLOCK. [S1] Sources: [S1] https://github.com/ethereum/execution-specs/blob/5ddb904fa7ba27daeff423e78466744c51e8cb6a/src/ethereum/forks/osaka/fork.py
transitions: 1. Validate header envelope and enforce MAX_RLP_BLOCK_SIZE before execution. [S1][S2] Sources: [S1] https://github.com/ethereum/execution-specs/blob/5ddb904fa7ba27daeff423e78466744c51e8cb6a/src/ethereum/forks/osaka/fork.py, [S2] https://eips.ethereum.org/EIPS/eip-7934

2. Iterate transactions, running Osaka validation then EVM execution with updated gas accounting and requests tracking. [S1][S2] Sources: [S1] https://github.com/ethereum/execution-specs/blob/5ddb904fa7ba27daeff423e78466744c51e8cb6a/src/ethereum/forks/osaka/transactions.py, [S2] https://github.com/ethereum/execution-specs/blob/5ddb904fa7ba27daeff423e78466744c51e8cb6a/src/ethereum/forks/osaka/fork.py

3. Seal block outputs, recompute trie roots, logs bloom, blob metrics, and aggregate requests_hash for CL consumption. [S1][S2] Sources: [S1] https://github.com/ethereum/execution-specs/blob/5ddb904fa7ba27daeff423e78466744c51e8cb6a/src/ethereum/forks/osaka/fork.py, [S2] https://github.com/ethereum/execution-specs/blob/5ddb904fa7ba27daeff423e78466744c51e8cb6a/src/ethereum/forks/osaka/requests.py

architecture.data_flow_diagram:
```mermaid
graph TD
  FCU[CL forkchoiceUpdated] -->|payloadAttributes| Builder
  Builder -->|transactions| TxPool
  TxPool -->|validated tx| EL[Osaka State Transition]
  EL -->|requests_hash, receipts| EngineAPI
  EL -->|blob_gas_used/excess| FCU
``` [S1][S2] Sources: [S1] https://github.com/ethereum/execution-specs/blob/5ddb904fa7ba27daeff423e78466744c51e8cb6a/src/ethereum/forks/osaka/fork.py, [S2] https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/blob/340d23358a0cdb4f4b547226dadd36d1cf775f20/specs/fulu/beacon-chain.md

normative_spec.title: Transaction Validation under Osaka [S1] Sources: [S1] https://github.com/ethereum/execution-specs/blob/5ddb904fa7ba27daeff423e78466744c51e8cb6a/src/ethereum/forks/osaka/transactions.py

[S1] Sources: [S1] https://github.com/ethereum/execution-specs/blob/5ddb904fa7ba27daeff423e78466744c51e8cb6a/src/ethereum/forks/osaka/transactions.py

Blob bundle metadata when transaction type requires [S1] Sources: [S1] https://github.com/ethereum/execution-specs/blob/5ddb904fa7ba27daeff423e78466744c51e8cb6a/src/ethereum/forks/osaka/transactions.py
procedure: 1. Decode tx type; reject unknown prefixes or missing authorizations for SetCode transactions. [S1][S2] Sources: [S1] https://github.com/ethereum/execution-specs/blob/5ddb904fa7ba27daeff423e78466744c51e8cb6a/src/ethereum/forks/osaka/transactions.py, [S2] https://github.com/ethereum/execution-specs/blob/5ddb904fa7ba27daeff423e78466744c51e8cb6a/src/ethereum/forks/osaka/vm/eoa_delegation.py

2. Compute intrinsic gas with calldata floor pricing and ensure tx.gas covers both intrinsic and floor costs. [S1] Sources: [S1] https://github.com/ethereum/execution-specs/blob/5ddb904fa7ba27daeff423e78466744c51e8cb6a/src/ethereum/forks/osaka/transactions.py

3. Enforce TX_MAX_GAS_LIMIT = 16,777,216, nonce < 2**64-1, signature bounds, and balance sufficiency. 4. For blob transactions, verify versioned hashes, max blob count, fee caps, and inclusion of blob data commitments. [S1] Sources: [S1] https://github.com/ethereum/execution-specs/blob/5ddb904fa7ba27daeff423e78466744c51e8cb6a/src/ethereum/forks/osaka/transactions.py

errors.when: Blob fee caps below required base price or missing blob data. [S1][S2] Sources: [S1] https://github.com/ethereum/execution-specs/blob/5ddb904fa7ba27daeff423e78466744c51e8cb6a/src/ethereum/forks/osaka/transactions.py, [S2] https://github.com/ethereum/execution-specs/blob/5ddb904fa7ba27daeff423e78466744c51e8cb6a/src/ethereum/forks/osaka/fork.py

normative_spec.rationale: Protects execution layer from DoS and consensus splits by enforcing deterministic bounds on gas, calldata pricing, and blob inclusion semantics. [S1][S2][S3] Sources: [S1] https://github.com/ethereum/execution-specs/blob/5ddb904fa7ba27daeff423e78466744c51e8cb6a/src/ethereum/forks/osaka/transactions.py, [S2] https://eips.ethereum.org/EIPS/eip-7825, [S3] https://eips.ethereum.org/EIPS/eip-7918

normative_spec.title: PeerDAS Tx Wrapper Cell Proofs Validation [EIP7594] Sources: [EIP7594] https://eips.ethereum.org/EIPS/eip-7594
normative_spec.summary: Tx gossip wrapper MUST include cell proofs for all extended cells per blob. Clients MUST verify count and batch-verify proofs against commitments before admitting tx to pool.
preconditions: PeerDAS-enabled tx gossip wrapper present in PooledTransactions response. [EIP7594] Sources: [EIP7594] https://eips.ethereum.org/EIPS/eip-7594
inputs: tx_payload_body, wrapper_version=1, blobs, commitments, cell_proofs [EIP7594] Sources: [EIP7594] https://eips.ethereum.org/EIPS/eip-7594
procedure: 1. Ensure len(blob_versioned_hashes) == len(blobs) == len(commitments).
2. Ensure len(cell_proofs) == CELLS_PER_EXT_BLOB * len(blobs).
3. Verify commitments hash to versioned hashes.
4. Compute extension cells for each blob; batch-verify all cell proofs via verify_cell_kzg_proof_batch.
postconditions: Wrapper accepted only if proofs and lengths match; otherwise tx is dropped.

errors.effect: Drop tx and penalize peer in devp2p scoring

normative_spec.rationale: Shift expensive proof generation off the proposer; keep verification cheap and safe. [EIP7594] Sources: [EIP7594] https://eips.ethereum.org/EIPS/eip-7594

normative_spec.title: Block Import and Requests Hashing [S1][S2] Sources: [S1] https://github.com/ethereum/execution-specs/blob/5ddb904fa7ba27daeff423e78466744c51e8cb6a/src/ethereum/forks/osaka/fork.py, [S2] https://github.com/ethereum/execution-specs/blob/5ddb904fa7ba27daeff423e78466744c51e8cb6a/src/ethereum/forks/osaka/requests.py
normative_spec.summary: During block import, nodes must cap encoded block size, recompute trie roots, and build requests_hash from deposit, withdrawal, and consolidation side-effects before sealing the header. [S1][S2] Sources: [S1] https://github.com/ethereum/execution-specs/blob/5ddb904fa7ba27daeff423e78466744c51e8cb6a/src/ethereum/forks/osaka/fork.py, [S2] https://github.com/ethereum/execution-specs/blob/5ddb904fa7ba27daeff423e78466744c51e8cb6a/src/ethereum/forks/osaka/requests.py
preconditions: Parent block finalized and Osaka fork rules active. [S1] Sources: [S1] https://github.com/ethereum/execution-specs/blob/5ddb904fa7ba27daeff423e78466744c51e8cb6a/src/ethereum/forks/osaka/fork.py

Block body includes receipts for deposit contract events. [S1] Sources: [S1] https://github.com/ethereum/execution-specs/blob/5ddb904fa7ba27daeff423e78466744c51e8cb6a/src/ethereum/forks/osaka/requests.py
inputs: Block header, transactions, withdrawals, receipts tries [S1] Sources: [S1] https://github.com/ethereum/execution-specs/blob/5ddb904fa7ba27daeff423e78466744c51e8cb6a/src/ethereum/forks/osaka/fork.py
Execution receipts with deposit logs [S1] Sources: [S1] https://github.com/ethereum/execution-specs/blob/5ddb904fa7ba27daeff423e78466744c51e8cb6a/src/ethereum/forks/osaka/requests.py
procedure: 1. Reject blocks whose RLP encoding exceeds MAX_RLP_BLOCK_SIZE (10,485,760 - 2,097,152 safety margin). [S1][S2] Sources: [S1] https://github.com/ethereum/execution-specs/blob/5ddb904fa7ba27daeff423e78466744c51e8cb6a/src/ethereum/forks/osaka/fork.py, [S2] https://eips.ethereum.org/EIPS/eip-7934

2. Apply transactions and withdrawals, updating state, blob metrics, and receipts trie; verify gas used and roots match header values. [S1] Sources: [S1] https://github.com/ethereum/execution-specs/blob/5ddb904fa7ba27daeff423e78466744c51e8cb6a/src/ethereum/forks/osaka/fork.py

3. Parse deposit logs into request payloads, compute SHA2-based requests_hash, and compare to header field. [S1][S2][EIP7685] Sources: [S1] https://github.com/ethereum/execution-specs/blob/5ddb904fa7ba27daeff423e78466744c51e8cb6a/src/ethereum/forks/osaka/requests.py, [S2] https://github.com/ethereum/execution-specs/blob/5ddb904fa7ba27daeff423e78466744c51e8cb6a/src/ethereum/forks/osaka/fork.py, [EIP7685] https://eips.ethereum.org/EIPS/eip-7685

algorithms.purpose: Compute blob gas price from excess_blob_gas with enforced execution-cost floor.
algorithms.notes: Ensure monotonic price evolution and prevent underflow/overflow when updating excess_blob_gas.

interfaces.name: eth_config [EIP7910] Sources: [EIP7910] https://eips.ethereum.org/EIPS/eip-7910
interfaces.response_schema: Returns fork versioning, blob schedule, and gas caps keyed by fork name.
interfaces.notes: Clients must surface Osaka gas caps and blob schedule for tooling parity.

interfaces.name: engine_newPayloadV3 [S1][S2] Sources: [S1] https://github.com/ethereum/execution-specs/blob/5ddb904fa7ba27daeff423e78466744c51e8cb6a/src/ethereum/forks/osaka/fork.py, [S2] https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/blob/340d23358a0cdb4f4b547226dadd36d1cf775f20/specs/fulu/beacon-chain.md
interfaces.sequence_diagram:
```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
  CL->>EL: engine_newPayloadV3(payload, versioned_hashes, parent_beacon_root, execution_requests)
  EL-->>CL: VALID
  CL->>EL: engine_forkchoiceUpdatedV3(state, payloadAttributes)
  EL-->>CL: PayloadStatus(executionStatus, blobStatus)
```
interfaces.notes: Osaka must verify blob metrics and requests_hash before acknowledging payload to Fulu.

wire_protocol.notes: Osaka references eth/69 history expiry and relies on CL PeerDAS topics for **data_column_sidecar** propagation handled in Fulu networking. [S2] Sources: [S2] https://ethereum.github.io/consensus-specs/specs/fulu/p2p-interface/

constants.name: TX_MAX_GAS_LIMIT
constants.notes: Defines the 2**24 cap enforced during validation. [EIP7825] Sources: [EIP7825] https://eips.ethereum.org/EIPS/eip-7825

constants.name: MAX_RLP_BLOCK_SIZE
constants.notes: 10,485,760 byte hard cap minus 2,097,152 safety margin per Osaka. [EIP7934] Sources: [EIP7934] https://eips.ethereum.org/EIPS/eip-7934

constants.name: MAX_BLOBS_PER_BLOCK
constants.notes: Consensus-level per-block blob cap (Deneb/4844). Enforced by CL via blob parameters schedule. EL must reflect metrics in headers.

constants.name: MAX_BLOBS_PER_TX
constants.notes: Per-transaction blob cap introduced by PeerDAS/EIP-7594; clients MUST enforce at pool, network, and block production. [EIP7594] Sources: [EIP7594] https://eips.ethereum.org/EIPS/eip-7594

constants.name: GAS_P256VERIFY
constants.notes: Fixed gas charged before P256 signature verification.

invariants: Engine must emit requests_hash consistent with parsed deposit events.
MODEXP execution must respect tightened bounds to avoid underpriced inputs.
CLZ opcode returns 256 when operand is zero, ensuring consensus alignment.

user_flows.title: [Client] Osaka Payload Build
preconditions: Osaka rules activated and Engine API authenticated.
steps: 1. Tx pool filters incoming tx via OSK-TX-VALIDATION; for blob tx, applies OSK-PEERDAS-CELL-PROOFS and MAX_BLOBS_PER_TX.
2. Builder selects transactions, applies state transition, and records requests outputs.
3. Engine returns payload via get_payload including blob metrics.
4. Consensus layer submits engine_newPayloadV3 and awaits VALID status.
postconditions: Payload ready for forkchoiceUpdated with matching blob and request commitments.

worked_examples.title: Calldata Floor Gas Rejection
worked_examples.scenario: User submits a blobless transaction with 1,024 non-zero calldata bytes.
worked_examples.given: Intrinsic gas = 21,000; calldata floor adds 4,096 tokens * 10 gas.
worked_examples.when: If supplied gas < 61,000, validation raises InsufficientTransactionGasError.
worked_examples.then: Transaction dropped before execution, preventing state changes.

edge_cases: SetCode transactions without at least one authorization MUST trigger EmptyAuthorizationListError and fail.
Blob transaction carrying >6 versioned hashes MUST raise BlobCountExceededError.
P256VERIFY input shorter than 160 bytes MUST return empty output without reverting.

compatibility.cross_layer: Osaka exposes requests_hash and blob gas metrics that Fulu verifies via engine_newPayload/process_execution_payload, so both layers must agree on hash construction and blob limits.

since_previous.summary: Refactored fork metadata into namespace package, clarifying Osaka module layout.
Reorganized forks directory to prepare for Osaka inclusion.

breaking_changes: Enforced TX_MAX_GAS_LIMIT per EIP-7825 requires client config updates.
MAX_RLP_BLOCK_SIZE enforcement per EIP-7934 affects block production and sync.
Mandatory requests_hash field couples EL outputs to CL processing per EIP-7685.

bug_bounty.scope: Execution clients implementing Osaka, executable spec, and associated test vectors are in scope.
bug_bounty.impact: Consensus faults, payload invalidation, or DoS due to Osaka gas, blob, or requests handling qualify for rewards.
bug_bounty.exclusions: UI, telemetry, or documentation-only findings remain out of scope.
bug_bounty.reproduction: Proofs must include reproducible payload or transaction demonstrating Osaka rule violation on supported clients.
bug_bounty.reporting: Report privately via bounty.ethereum.org or bounty@ethereum.org with optional PGP per EF policy.
bug_bounty.rewards: Rewards follow EF tiers scaled by consensus impact and ease of exploitation.

forks.name: Fulu

architecture.overview: Fulu augments the beacon chain with PeerDAS data column sampling, proposer lookahead storage, and extended execution payload validation including blob schedule control and execution_requests forwarding.

components.name: Beacon State
components.description: Extends BeaconState with proposer_lookahead array, blob parameters, and execution payload header fields for PeerDAS enforcement.

components.name: PeerDAS Core
components.description: Defines custody groups, sampling requirements, DataColumnSidecar format, and reconstruction helpers for erasure-coded blob data.

components.name: Networking
components.description: Upgrades gossip, Req/Resp, and discovery protocols with data_column_sidecar topics, custody_group_count metadata, and Status v2 earliest_available_slot.

components.name: Validator Duties
components.description: Adjusts custody obligations, payload requests via GetPayloadResponse, and sidecar publishing/retention for validators.

state_machines.name: process_execution_payload
outputs: Notification to execution engine via verify_and_notify_new_payload
invariants: execution_engine.verify_and_notify_new_payload must succeed or block processing aborts.
transitions: 1. Check parent hash, prev_randao, and timestamp consistency.
2. Enforce blob commitment limit via blob parameters schedule.
3. Call execution engine with versioned hashes and execution_requests, then cache payload header.

architecture.data_flow_diagram:
```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
  Validator->>CL: Propose beacon block + data_column_sidecars
  CL->>EL: engine_newPayloadV3(payload, versioned_hashes, execution_requests)
  EL-->>CL: VALID
  Peer->>Validator: data_column_sidecar gossip
  Validator-->>Peers: Req/Resp DataColumnSidecarsByRange
```

normative_spec.title: PeerDAS Custody Sampling
normative_spec.summary: Nodes must advertise custody_group_count, join corresponding subnets, and sample at least SAMPLES_PER_SLOT columns, escalating to reconstruction if coverage drops.
preconditions: Node has computed custody groups via get_custody_groups.
procedure: 1. Determine custody groups and columns using get_custody_groups(node_id, custody_group_count) and compute_columns_for_custody_group(g).
2. Join data_column_sidecar_{subnet_id} topics for assigned groups and begin sampling >= max(SAMPLES_PER_SLOT, custody_group_count).
3. Validate sidecars via verify_data_column_sidecar, inclusion proof, and KZG batch verification before marking samples available.
4. If coverage < threshold, reconstruct missing columns via recover_matrix and cross-seed if subscribed.
postconditions: Node updates custody records and exposes availability to peers.

errors.when: Sidecar fails structural / inclusion / KZG verification.
errors.effect: Reject gossip message and penalize peer per scoring rules.

errors.when: Node fails to fetch required samples per slot.
errors.effect: Trigger recovery or mark column unavailable impacting attestations.

normative_spec.rationale: Ensures data availability guarantees and resilience against partial sidecar loss.

normative_spec.title: Deterministic Custody Assignment
normative_spec.summary: Custody groups and columns MUST be selected deterministically from node_id and custody_group_count so any peer can reproduce a node’s obligations.
procedure: 1. groups = get_custody_groups(node_id, custody_group_count)  // unique, sorted, prefix-stable as count increases.
2. For each g in groups, columns_g = compute_columns_for_custody_group(g).
3. sampling_size = max(SAMPLES_PER_SLOT, custody_group_count); sample groups' columns each slot.
postconditions: Peers can re-compute a node's custody set from advertised cgc and node_id.
Custody set is stable across restarts given stable node_id.

errors.effect: Implementation bug; MUST NOT happen (assert uniqueness).

normative_spec.rationale: Public, deterministic selection prevents bias and enables network-wide auditing of custody coverage.

normative_spec.title: Reed–Solomon Matrix Reconstruction and Cross‑Seeding
normative_spec.summary: If a node has ≥ 50% of the columns, it SHOULD reconstruct the full data matrix and treat reconstructed columns as if received via gossip, including anti‑equivocation updates.
preconditions: Have partial matrix entries (cells + proofs) for at least ceil(NUMBER_OF_COLUMNS/2) columns across all rows
procedure: 1. Use recover_matrix(partial_matrix, blob_count) which calls recover_cells_and_kzg_proofs per row.
2. For each reconstructed column: (a) package DataColumnSidecar with kzg_commitments and proofs; (b) if subscribed to its subnet, gossip it; else mark availability for req/resp.
3. Optionally randomize reconstruction delay to reduce thundering-herd; bound CPU.
postconditions: All columns available locally; reconstructed ones exposed to peers according to subscription.

errors.effect: Abort and quarantine inputs; do not gossip reconstructed sidecars.

normative_spec.rationale: 50%+ column availability suffices to recover all rows; cross‑seeding stabilizes availability during proposer failures.

normative_spec.title: Subnet Mapping for Data Columns
normative_spec.summary: Each column index maps deterministically to a gossip subnet id.
procedure: 1. subnet_id = column_index % DATA_COLUMN_SIDECAR_SUBNET_COUNT.
2. Publish/accept DataColumnSidecar only on its correct subnet; otherwise REJECT.
postconditions: No cross‑subnet leakage; predictable routing and mesh construction.
normative_spec.rationale: Ensures balanced dissemination and prevents partitioning due to inconsistent routing.

normative_spec.title: Distributed Blob Publishing (DBP)
normative_spec.summary: If proposer fails or is late publishing columns, honest nodes SHOULD fetch blobs from local EL via engine_getBlobsV2, convert to columns, and import/gossip them as if received from gossip, with anti‑equivocation updates.
preconditions: Received a valid beacon_block or some DataColumnSidecar for the block
Local EL exposes matching blobs for the block’s KZG commitments
procedure: 1. Query engine_getBlobsV2 immediately after receiving the block/sidecar.
2. Convert blobs to columns; obtain/derive KZG proofs for required cells.
3. Import as DataColumnSidecar: apply same validation and anti‑equivocation as gossip path; publish only if subscribed to the subnet.
postconditions: Columns available for attesters; network recovers from missing publisher.

errors.when: EL returns a subset/mismatch of blobs
errors.effect: Do not import; continue normal sampling/reconstruction.

normative_spec.rationale: Improves liveness by letting non‑proposers backfill columns when proposer is faulty or slow.

normative_spec.title: Proposer Lookahead Maintenance
normative_spec.summary: Beacon nodes must maintain proposer_lookahead across epochs and verify proposer indices for sidecar validation.
preconditions: State upgraded via upgrade_to_fulu with proposer_lookahead initialized.
inputs: Current epoch and custody scheduling parameters
procedure: 1. During epoch processing, run process_proposer_lookahead to refresh indices for (MIN_SEED_LOOKAHEAD+1)*SLOTS_PER_EPOCH slots.
2. When validating data_column_sidecar, confirm proposer_index matches lookahead entry for slot.
3. If mismatch detected, queue sidecar until proposer shuffling confirmed or reject if proven invalid.
postconditions: Lookahead array stays aligned with fork choice and custody validations.

errors.when: proposer_index differs from expected index for slot
errors.effect: Client ignores sidecar until proposer mapping recomputed.

normative_spec.rationale: Provides deterministic proposer verification for PeerDAS sidecars and attestation duties.

algorithms.name: Custody Assignment
algorithms.purpose: Assign custody groups and columns per node deterministically.
algorithms.notes: Avoids duplicates, supports super-full nodes, and scales with NUMBER_OF_CUSTODY_GROUPS.

interfaces.name: GET /eth/v2/beacon/blocks/{slot}
interfaces.notes: Responses include blob_kzg_commitments and execution_requests needed for Osaka verification.

interfaces.name: DataColumnSidecarsByRange v1
interfaces.notes: Serves DataColumnSidecar payloads over SSZ chunks within MIN_EPOCHS_FOR_DATA_COLUMN_SIDECARS_REQUESTS window.

wire_protocol.notes: Nodes must validate subnet routing, anti-equivocation, proposer signature, inclusion proof, and KZG proofs before forwarding sidecars.

constants.name: SAMPLES_PER_SLOT
constants.notes: Minimum samples honest node must download each slot.

constants.name: NUMBER_OF_CUSTODY_GROUPS
constants.notes: Total custody groups available across PeerDAS network.

constants.name: NUMBER_OF_COLUMNS
constants.notes: Columns in the extended data matrix; equals CELLS_PER_EXT_BLOB.

constants.name: DATA_COLUMN_SIDECAR_SUBNET_COUNT
constants.notes: Number of gossip subnets dedicated to column distribution.

constants.name: MIN_EPOCHS_FOR_DATA_COLUMN_SIDECARS_REQUESTS
constants.notes: Retention window for serving DataColumnSidecar requests.

invariants: earliest_available_slot in Status v2 must reflect oldest stored data column sidecars.
Custody requirement for validator-attached nodes scales with effective balance.

user_flows.title: [Validator] PeerDAS Sampling Cycle
steps: 1. At slot start, compute sampling groups using total_node_balance derived requirement.
2. Subscribe to required data_column_sidecar subnets and request missing columns.
3. Verify sidecars and record custody proofs; reconstruct if partial coverage.
4. Publish attestations only if sampling thresholds satisfied.
postconditions: Validator ready to attest with availability evidence or abstain if data incomplete.

worked_examples.title: Sampling with Partial Sidecar Loss
worked_examples.scenario: Node misses two custody groups due to slow gossip.
worked_examples.when: Node retrieves 6 sidecars via gossip and requests remaining 2 via DataColumnSidecarsByRange.
worked_examples.then: If Req/Resp succeeds before attestation deadline, node attests; otherwise it must abstain to avoid invalid vote.

edge_cases: Sidecar with empty kzg_commitments list must be rejected before gossip propagation.
Node advertising reduced custody_group_count must still serve historical higher count.
BlobSidecarsByRange requests beyond retention window may return empty without peer penalty.
If proposer shuffling is not yet confirmed, clients SHOULD queue (not reject) sidecars until lookahead aligns, to avoid false negatives.

compatibility.cross_layer: Fulu verifies Osaka requests_hash, blob_gas_used, and excess_blob_gas through engine APIs while enforcing blob schedule via get_blob_parameters.

since_previous.summary: Adjusted pyspec test generation to gate ssz_static creation in generator mode, keeping PeerDAS fixtures lean.
Forwarded verbose flag to pyspec rebuild step, aiding debug of Fulu generators.

breaking_changes: DataColumnSidecar gossip replaces blob_sidecar topics, requiring networking upgrades.
Status v2 earliest_available_slot field mandated for PeerDAS availability tracking.
Validators must satisfy upgraded custody requirements tied to effective balances.

bug_bounty.scope: Consensus clients implementing Fulu networking, PeerDAS sampling, and proposer lookahead fall within EF bounty scope.
bug_bounty.impact: Findings causing consensus splits, invalid availability votes, or custody bypass qualify for higher tiers.
bug_bounty.exclusions: Cosmetic issues in specs or non-security documentation remain out of scope.
bug_bounty.reproduction: Reports must include reproducible beacon node traces or failing test vectors demonstrating PeerDAS or lookahead faults.
bug_bounty.reporting: Submit via bounty.ethereum.org or bounty@ethereum.org following responsible disclosure timelines.
bug_bounty.rewards: Rewards mirror EF consensus bounty tiers scaled by slashable fault risk.

interfaces.name: Engine API / Fork Choice
preconditions: EL and CL share JWT auth and advertise matching fork versions.
interfaces.sequence_diagram:
```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
  CL->>EL: engine_newPayloadV3(payload, hashes, parent_root, execution_requests)
  EL-->>CL: PayloadStatus(VALID|INVALID, blobStatus)
  CL->>EL: engine_forkchoiceUpdatedV3(state, payloadAttributes)
  EL-->>CL: PayloadId / SYNCING
```
errors: SYNCING when EL behind finalized head.
INVALID_BLOCK_HASH when requests_hash or blob metrics mismatch.
interfaces.timeouts: Clients should retry forkchoiceUpdated if EL does not respond within slot time, backing off exponentially.
interfaces.notes: Fulu relies on Osaka to reject invalid blob bundles and publish execution_requests for CL processing.

cross_layer.data_availability: Osaka enforces blob fee floors and per-tx/blob limits while Fulu spreads columns across custody groups; together they guarantee availability via pricing and PeerDAS sampling.

security_requirements.description: Execution clients must enforce Osaka gas caps, block size, and requests hashing before exposing payloads to consensus.
Consensus clients must validate PeerDAS sidecars and meet sampling thresholds before attesting to block availability.
Engine API interactions must keep CL and EL fork choice aligned, rejecting payloads with mismatched blob metrics or requests_hash.
Blob fee computation must remain monotonic with the Osaka minimum to prevent economic attacks or underflows.
Proposer lookahead must be reproducible from beacon state so peers can detect equivocation or invalid sidecars.
Enforce MAX_BLOBS_PER_TX (6) per EIP‑7594 at pool, network receive, and block assembly.
Gossip validation MUST include subnet check, proposer sig, inclusion proof, and batch KZG verification before forwarding.

attack_paths.title: Proposer/EL/Subnet gossips malformed or adversarial DataColumnSidecar
attack_paths.description: Invalid structure, wrong subnet, bad inclusion proof, or invalid KZG proofs causing verification failure or CPU spikes.
checkpoints: Reject on wrong subnet before heavy checks
O(1) structural checks before batch KZG
Cap batch sizes and timeouts; penalize peers on failure
Cache inclusion proof tuple per block to avoid rework
mitigations: Apply full gossip validation gate (structure→subnet→parent seen→proposer sig→inclusion→KZG)
Bound anti‑equivocation cache by time/keys

attack_paths.title: RS reconstruction inconsistency and crash triggers
attack_paths.description: Incorrect column indices or mixing cells from other columns triggers reconstruction failure, KZG mismatches, or crashes.
checkpoints: Begin reconstruction only when columns >= ceil(NUMBER_OF_COLUMNS/2)
Re-verify KZG against the recover_matrix return value
Assert column index matches cell index as an invariant
mitigations: Assert invariants before and after reconstruction
Sort inputs and deduplicate

attack_paths.title: Custody calculation bias or divergence (misassignment causing slashing, missed rewards, or partitions)
attack_paths.description: Implementation drift in get_custody_groups or unstable node_id derivation causes assignments to diverge from other clients.
checkpoints: Stabilize node_id (derive consistently from ENR or peerId)
Assert get_custody_groups outputs are deduplicated and sorted
Validate indices 0..NUMBER_OF_CUSTODY_GROUPS-1 with cross-client vectors
mitigations: Log and monitor the node self-assignment hash at startup
Verify prefix-stable behavior when the custody group configuration (cgc) changes

attack_paths.title: DBP abuse (seeding incorrect blobs from the EL without validation)
attack_paths.description: Blindly trusting engine_getBlobsV2 responses spreads invalid columns.
checkpoints: Apply the same validation and deduplication as gossip to DBP-generated columns
Publish only to subscribed subnets
Always check EL response versioned hash and commitment lengths and consistency
mitigations: Validate DataColumnSidecars before ingesting
Abort DBP and fall back to standard DAS or reconstruction when EL responses are missing

attack_paths.title: Subnetwork mapping implementation drift (mod and floor discrepancies)
attack_paths.description: Differences in column-to-subnet mapping can partition the network.
checkpoints: Unit-test compute_subnet_for_data_column_sidecar with table lookups across all 128 columns
Specify identifier types and bounds explicitly (unsigned with no wraparound)
mitigations: Reference DATA_COLUMN_SIDECAR_SUBNET_COUNT from the shared definition
Verify end-to-end cross-client send and receive consistency

attack_paths.title: Batch KZG verification regression or DoS
attack_paths.description: Huge batches or malformed inputs induce latency spikes.
checkpoints: Perform O(1) checks that sidecar column counts match commitment and proof lengths
Enforce batch size caps and wall-clock abort conditions
Confirm domain separation for random challenges
mitigations: Parallelize verification and prioritize by slot deadlines
Lower scores for misbehaving peers

attack_paths.title: Anti-equivocation cache memory pressure
attack_paths.description: Repeated transmissions for the same (slot, proposer, column) tuple inflate memory.
checkpoints: Bound key TTL and the maximum entry count
Define clear state transitions from accept to eviction
mitigations: Use fixed memory pools or ring buffers
Penalize peers and ignore early

attack_paths.title: Req/Resp request flooding (DataColumnSidecarsByRange)
attack_paths.description: Out-of-range or excessive requests consume bandwidth.
checkpoints: Enforce MIN_EPOCHS_FOR_DATA_COLUMN_SIDECARS_REQUESTS
Apply MAX_REQUEST_DATA_COLUMN_SIDECARS
Respond with ResourceUnavailable appropriately and downgrade
mitigations: Rate-limit and cap concurrent processing
Abort early when fork choice changes

attack_paths.title: MAX_BLOBS_PER_TX violations slipping through
attack_paths.description: Allows more than six blobs per transaction.
checkpoints: Apply the same guard at pool intake, network admission, and block construction
Test error paths for exception names and return codes
mitigations: Reuse a unified validation function
Expose violation counts via metrics

attack_paths.title: Cell array ordering or endianness mix-ups
attack_paths.description: Out-of-order compute_cells execution causes verification failures.
checkpoints: Add boundary tests for BYTES_PER_CELL and CELLS_PER_EXT_BLOB
Run cross-implementation cell hash and reversibility tests
mitigations: Use a shared KZG library such as c-kzg-4844
Validate row and column alignment with static test vectors

attack_paths.title: Proposer validation flapping (false rejects)
attack_paths.description: Rejecting before shuffle stabilizes causes availability loss.
checkpoints: Follow the spec flow: QUEUE (IGNORE) then verify later
Guarantee processing order when resynchronizing lookahead and fork choice
mitigations: Retry fetching branch parents with deadlines
Emit state root and branch values to audit logs

attack_paths.title: Custody subnet saturation and listener starvation
attack_paths.description: Adversaries concentrate peers on specific custody subnets to absorb sampling requests or leave subnets without listeners, delaying blob availability confirmations.
checkpoints: Enforce per-subnet inbound/outbound peer caps and rotate peers when sample success ratio drops
Emit metrics/alerts when a custody subnet peer count falls below the redundancy threshold
Reassign or rejoin custody subnets proactively when repeated timeouts occur
mitigations: Randomize peer replacement across custody groups each epoch
Drop or deprioritize peers that exceed request-failure limits

attack_paths.title: PeerDAS request backpressure bypass
attack_paths.description: Flooding DataColumnSidecars or cell sampling requests without adequate backpressure causes goroutine/thread exhaustion or memory spikes.
checkpoints: Rate-limit concurrent column-sidecar requests per peer and per slot
Attach cancellable contexts and release resources on disconnect or timeout
Track outstanding work; refuse new requests when queue depth exceeds safe limits
mitigations: Share a bounded worker pool across DAS handlers
Record peer penalties for repeated cancellations or incomplete responses

attack_paths.title: Stale fragment replay loop
attack_paths.description: Rebroadcasting expired column fragments or extended cells can keep validators busy revalidating obsolete data until TTL rules eject them.
checkpoints: Assign per-slot TTL and drop sidecars older than the spec window before validation
Maintain deduplication keys (slot, proposer, column, chunk) with bounded TTL
Stop retrying peers that repeatedly deliver stale or duplicated fragments
mitigations: Emit metrics for stale fragment drops
Escalate to reconstruction requests when gossip sources stale repeatedly

attack_paths.title: cell_proofs wrapper integrity bypass
attack_paths.description: Mixed or truncated cell proofs combined with valid blob commitments bypass integrity checks when wrapper length validation is incomplete.
checkpoints: Verify cell_proofs count equals CELLS_PER_EXT_BLOB * len(blobs)
Ensure each proof index aligns with its blob commitment before batch verification
Reject unknown or unsupported wrapper_version before decoding
mitigations: Fuzz wrapper decoding with mismatched lengths
Keep cross-client test vectors for wrapper_version evolution

attack_paths.title: Versioned hash compression drift
attack_paths.description: Differences in kzg_to_versioned_hash prefixing or G1 compression cause mismatch between commitments and versioned hashes across clients.
checkpoints: Run canonical test vectors for kzg_to_versioned_hash across languages
Ensure prefix and truncation logic conform to 4844 spec
Validate codec output matches blob header version expectations before acceptance
mitigations: Centralize the conversion routine and reuse in EL/CL pipelines
Add regression tests for edge-case commitments (infinity, small-order)

attack_paths.title: Invalid KZG point acceptance regression
attack_paths.description: Relaxed deserialisation of G1/G2 points allows invalid commitments to be accepted, leading to consensus splits.
checkpoints: Force deserialisation routines to reject points not on the curve or outside the subgroup
Fuzz decoding with known invalid point sets per release
Abort verification when library returns malformed points instead of fallback acceptance
mitigations: Pin kzg libraries to releases with subgroup checks
Add dynamic health checks that spot divergence after deserialisation

attack_paths.title: Sampling bias under partial availability
attack_paths.description: An adversary withholds roughly half the columns and biases sampling so many validators hit only available regions, delaying reconstruction.
checkpoints: Reroll sampling targets when repeated timeouts occur
Mix peers across operators and custody groups before finalising availability
Escalate to full reconstruction once minority of samples fail twice
mitigations: Track peer diversity and enforce minimum distinct operators per sampling window
Instrument sampling outcome metrics (success rate, retry counts)

attack_paths.title: Finalized epoch sidecar gaps
attack_paths.description: Failure to serve or request DataColumnSidecars across finality boundaries prevents late joiners from reconstructing old blobs.
checkpoints: Expose DataColumnSidecarsByRoot for finalized epochs as required
Test finalized/unfinalized transitions end-to-end
Cache finalised sidecars on disk with retention policies
mitigations: Backfill missing finalized sidecars during warm-up
Alert when finalized retrieval fails repeatedly

attack_paths.title: Partial-bad cell flood
attack_paths.description: Sending many minimally corrupted cells forces repeated batch verification retries, exhausting CPU.
checkpoints: Abort batch verification on first failure and penalize sender
Set per-slot limits on failed batch retries
Separate healthy and faulty cells to avoid reprocessing good data repeatedly
mitigations: Maintain peer failure counters tied to invalid cell rate
Prioritize verification of distinct slots before retrying failed ones

attack_paths.title: Checkpoint sync restart inconsistency
attack_paths.description: Restarting after periods of non-finality can leave custody data or DAS caches inconsistent and block sampling.
checkpoints: Clear stale DAS cache entries on checkpoint sync
Validate database migrations and version markers before serving requests
Warm up required sidecars after restart before marking availability
mitigations: Run integration tests covering restart from checkpoint with partial availability
Log restart audits and refuse to serve until caches are repopulated

attack_paths.title: Devnet regression relapse
attack_paths.description: Historical PeerDAS devnet bugs reappear when patches regress, reintroducing custody isolation or reconstruction failures.
checkpoints: Add regression tests reproducing devnet-2/3/6 incidents (custody isolation, reconstructor stalls, listener holes)
Monitor metrics for early warning (peers per subnet, reconstruction lag, request failure rate)
Document remediation runbooks and verify alerts fire within one slot
mitigations: Keep invariant tests that replay archived devnet traces
Include devnet regression suites in CI before releases

attack_paths.title: Full-wrapper mempool flood
attack_paths.description: High-rate transactions with maximum blobs and cell proofs drive verification queues and memory to exhaustion before admission controls react.
checkpoints: Enforce per-slot and global limits on total blob and cell_proof payload accepted into the pool
Throttle parallel wrapper verification workers and drop excess
Record metrics for blob/cell proof backlog and alert on sustained growth
mitigations: Reuse verified wrapper fingerprints across transactions
Gracefully shed load once verification backlog exceeds threshold

attack_paths.title: Duplicate sidecar flood
attack_paths.description: Attackers spam identical or near-identical DataColumnSidecars on a custody subnet to exhaust receive queues and memory buffers.
checkpoints: Maintain deduplication keys (root, column, chunk) with bounded TTL
Cap simultaneous sidecar processing per (slot, column) and per peer
Downgrade peers whose duplicate ratio exceeds policy
mitigations: Track duplicate statistics per subnet
Drop late-arriving sidecars once quorum achieved

attack_paths.title: Incomplete sampling backlog
attack_paths.description: Adversaries deliver partial column responses and disconnect just before completion, causing unfinished sampling records to accumulate.
checkpoints: Evict incomplete sampling state immediately on timeout or peer switch
Limit concurrent incomplete sampling records per peer
Penalize peers that repeatedly produce partial responses
mitigations: Prefer peers with recent full-success history
Persist only successful samples to long-lived caches

attack_paths.title: Micro-reorg re-fetch thrash
attack_paths.description: Frequent small reorgs force repeated DataColumnSidecarsByRoot fetches, stressing memory and bandwidth if responses are not coalesced.
checkpoints: Bound concurrent ByRoot re-fetches and prefer ByRange when reorg depth >= 2
Release cached sidecars for superseded roots immediately
Debounce re-fetches when fork choice oscillates within a slot
mitigations: Stage re-fetch work through a bounded queue
Expose metrics for ByRoot retry counts

attack_paths.title: Adversarial compute_cells workload
attack_paths.description: Blobs crafted to trigger worst-case FFT/cache behavior keep compute_cells busy and stall downstream verification.
checkpoints: Benchmark compute_cells against adversarial vectors and abort when runtime exceeds configured budget
Schedule compute_cells on bounded worker pools with priority to current slot
Cache results keyed by blob commitment and reuse across requests
mitigations: Adopt shared polynomial libraries with constant-time configuration
Collect histograms of compute_cells latency to catch regressions

attack_paths.title: Commitment fingerprint evasion
attack_paths.description: Repeatedly reusing the same blob commitment across many transactions forces redundant verification when caches are weak or absent.
checkpoints: Maintain per-slot commitment fingerprints and skip re-verification
Reject pools that exceed blob reuse limits
Expose metrics for commitment reuse to operators
mitigations: Share verification results across mempool, gossip, and block builder
Score peers negatively for mass duplicate commitments

attack_paths.title: Incomplete sidecar persistence leak
attack_paths.description: Unfinished or superseded sidecars linger in memory or disk after restarts, consuming resources and confusing availability checks.
checkpoints: Purge incomplete DAS entries at startup and on checkpoint sync
Mark superseded roots and delete associated sidecar files promptly
Bound the number of cached partial sidecars per slot
mitigations: Add integrity scans for DAS storage
Refuse to serve availability until caches are sane

attack_paths.title: Logging/metrics amplification
attack_paths.description: Forged error conditions or duplicate requests trigger excessive logging or metrics updates, consuming CPU and disk bandwidth.
checkpoints: Rate-limit high cardinality logs tied to DAS failures
Batch metrics updates for repeated errors
Provide configuration to downgrade verbose logging under load
mitigations: Use structured sampling for repetitive messages
Monitor log volume and auto-throttle when exceeding limits
